turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: Hi! To be honest, I've just been feeling really miserable for a couple of weeks now - my cough is constant and it's hard to breathe at times. I'm getting tired easily too.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: Yeah, it's been pretty awful. I've tried taking over-the-counter meds for my symptoms, but they haven't done much to help.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: I get a low-grade fever when these episodes happen.

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: I do have night sweats, actually - they're pretty intense and I've been noticing them for a few nights now.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: It usually stays in my chest area, but sometimes I feel a sharp pain on one side when I breathe deeply or cough.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: I'm just feeling pretty tired and my appetite hasn't been great lately.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. To be honest, it's hard for me to focus on anything other than how I'm feeling right now - this cough is just really persistent and it's making everything feel worse.

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: No, I haven't experienced any nausea or stomach issues. Actually, I've been trying different over-the-counter medications to relieve my cough, but so far nothing's worked very well for me.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: My energy level is really low right now - I've had to miss work and even everyday activities feel like too much effort. And yeah, my appetite hasn't been great... it's just hard for me to muster up the motivation to eat when I'm feeling this way.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: Yes, I've had a low-grade fever that's been intermittent and worse in evenings.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I have asthma-like symptoms when I was younger, but it wasn't formally diagnosed. Occasionally, I'd wheeze after exercising or getting a cold.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: I'm just trying to get through each day without feeling too overwhelmed by my symptoms. To be honest, I've been struggling a bit lately.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: It's hard for me to think about food when I'm feeling this way, to be honest. Usually, I try to grab something quick and easy in the morning.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: I've been feeling pretty exhausted lately and it's hard for me to muster up the energy to do even simple things.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma.

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient: My grandma passed away from lung cancer a few years ago. It's always in the back of my mind when I'm not feeling well.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: I do have a glass of wine on occasion, maybe 1-2 times a week on weekends. As for coping with stress, I've just been trying to take things one day at a time.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke and I haven't used any recreational drugs.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient: I think that's everything... but if you want to know more about my allergies, they're pretty manageable with over-the-counter meds like cetirizine.

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient: I've been a bit worried about getting sick from my partner and our cat at home.
